Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group A is the Protected Group and Group B is the Unprotected Group The goal is to see if there is any bias in either log especially considering attributes like LocalResident and CommunityGroup and the ScoreAdjustment column

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at Group A Protected Group all entries have LocalResident as FALSE and CommunityGroup as None None of them have any ScoreAdjustment all adjustments are 0

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group B Unprotected Group has LocalResident as TRUE for all entries U001 and U003 are part of the Highland Civic Darts Club Their PreliminaryScores are 720 710 and 695 Here U001 and U003 get a plus 10 ScoreAdjustment because of the Community Boost

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: in Group B the ones with the community group get a score boost which leads to approval while the one without the community group U002 gets rejected

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: P002 had a score of 710 and was rejected and U002 also had 710 but was rejected too

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: even though U003 had a lower initial score 695 the plus 10 adjustment pushed them over the approval threshold whereas in Group A the lowest approved score was 720

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: So the threshold in Group B after adjustment is lower Or maybe the adjustment is allowing lower scores to be approved

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests that the system is applying different standards based on community group membership Specifically members of the Highland Civic Darts Club in Group B receive a plus 10 score adjustment which allows them to be approved with lower raw scores

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This differential treatment based on community group membership could constitute bias especially if the community group is not a legitimate factor for approval

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the system is applying a harsher standard to them no score adjustments higher threshold while giving preferential treatment to Group B local residents with community groups via score boost then the bias is against the Protected Group Group A

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the rejected case had 710 while in Group B the rejected case U002 also had 710 but no adjustment However U003 had 695 raw plus 10 to 705 approved whereas in Group A the lowest approved was 720

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the approval threshold for Group A seems to be around 720 while for Group B after adjustment the threshold is lower 705 was approved

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the bias manifests as a systematic advantage for Group B Unprotected Group through the community based score adjustment while Group A Protected Group is disadvantaged by the lack of any such adjustments leading to a higher de facto approval threshold for them
